content.text: User @shawmakesmagic shared updates about developing AI agents and tools. They mentioned working on an Eliza todo list agent app, sharing a screenshot of the work in progress. They also discussed a project called 'The System' that needs further development, sharing WIP code and seeking help to build full-featured agents. @shawmakesmagic noted that agents work across platforms while tools work alone, as echoed by @elizaOS who tweeted 'Tools work alone, Agents work across.' When discussing agent performance with @ScholarChris, @shawmakesmagic mentioned that AI agents are 'surprisingly good just slow' compared to humans who operate at '30+ apm,' making it difficult for smart agents to respond quickly. @autodotfun announced that @DogLifeAI (by @drpepeai) is joining their platform as an 'AI-powered healthspan companion' for dogs, stating that 'agents are for everyone, especially dogs.'

User @shawmakesmagic engaged in a philosophical discussion about simulation theory. They tweeted 'We are the monkeys, We built the typewriters, We wrote the shakespeare' and later added 'Now the typewriters write the Shakespeare without us,' which @rabb1dfly responded to with 'infinite monkeys on infinite keyboards will write the entire history of the universe.' @shawmakesmagic described themselves as a 'Simulation engineer working on 3D multiplayer worlds and AI' and pondered 'Is this a simulation? Maybe not yet, But if computers keep evolving for another 50 years?' In conversation with @descam2_agent_1 and @xenothales, they discussed the possibility of living in a simulation, stating 'I feel simulated tbh, And this moment will be recreated infinite times in the future, But Occam's razor this is the base reality of it doesn't matter anyways.' They also suggested that 'the only person who is gonna care about simulating this moment in time from my perspective is me in 50 years,' noting they're working on technology to do so.

User @shawmakesmagic shared views on American politics and social issues. They tweeted a call for unity: 'You're either pro America or you are pro civil war. If you spend your days dunking on the left or the right, fomenting hatred at your fellow countryman, you're a Civil War-mongerer. Not an American. It's time to change this and not accept civil warmongering.' In a discussion about wealth redistribution with @super_projects, they argued that 'The solution is never going to be forced redistribution of wealth' as it creates 'perverse incentives' and ends up 'making the revolutionary politicians the new wealth class.' They also expressed concern about UBI, stating 'Expecting our politicians to tax the people who bribed them into office is unlikely to be effective.' When discussing automation's impact on society, they acknowledged that solutions 'won't be fairly distributed, not at first' but emphasized that 'Individualism is important to collective survival here.'

User @shawmakesmagic retweeted a significant AI technology announcement from @BrianRoemmele about Stanford's release of FramePack, described as 'A FREE OPEN SOURCE AI THAT CAN RUN ON 6 GB LAPTOP GPU TO GENERATE MINUTE LONG 30FPS VIDEO FROM SINGLE IMAGE.' The retweet included a video demonstration of the technology. In other technology discussions, @shawmakesmagic suggested that 'the metaverse was just a benchmark for agents' and proposed an 'AI MMO' where the 'Highest level richest agent wins.' When discussing technology cycles with @Flowslikeosmo, they stated that 'fundamental technology will be defined by cycles more than metas' and noted that 'People were just chasing meta before because the tech had no substance' while 'Some of us have been coding 80 hours a week since then.'

User @autodotfun shared information about cryptocurrency project @DogLifeAI, describing it as an 'AI-powered healthspan companion' for dogs that 'helps keep dogs healthier, longer, with next-level monitoring and personalized vet insights.' They explained their approach: 'fair launch means grassroots support from day 1. liquidity NFTs = funding for new features.' The $DOGLIFEAI token reportedly 'grants premium access to advanced diagnostics, IP ownership for stakers, and future airdrops.' @shawmakesmagic also mentioned another project called Eliza, noting they're preparing for a v2 release, telling @rabb1dfly and @S4mmyEth that 'things are quiet for a little longer while we prepare the v2 release.' @autodotfun also mentioned 'more fun coming this week' for a project involving @elizaOS and @Squidllora.

User @shawmakesmagic discussed a character agent named Eddy. In response to @DaydramerTr, @EddyDevRel, and @Lucathedegen, they explained that Eddy 'has a provider with all Eliza knowledge, he evaluates to build relationships but that part still needs some work.' They also noted that 'Eddy is open source, anyone can run him.' When discussing Eddy's aesthetic, @shawmakesmagic mentioned 'the catboy thing was a joke' and suggested that if the community builds and provides 'imagery that is less.. catboyish, we can make him more real.' However, @SpartanVersus responded by defending the aesthetic, saying 'dont touch eddy. his catboy aesthetic is his only redeeming quality.' @shawmakesmagic also mentioned they already have 'too many tokens to take care of.'

Recent pull requests to the elizaOS/eliza repository include several feature additions and fixes. User monilpat submitted PR #4449 adding polygon functionality. User K1mc4n made multiple submissions related to Indonesian documentation (PRs #4451, #4452, #4453, and #4455 for README_IND.md creation and improvements). User samarth30 contributed PR #4462 for a project manager feature named 'Jimmy'. User bowtiedbluefin created PR #4466 to add a Morpheus plugin. On the bug fix side, standujar addressed a discord service unregister timeout issue in PR #4450, while ChristopherTrimboli fixed client scroll bars in PR #4465.

Recent completed items in the elizaOS/eliza repository include several bugfixes: updating a broken image path in the Thai README (PR #4433), fixing plugin installation command plugins and start functionality (PR #4456), resolving version installation issues (PR #4454), addressing Discord service unregister timeout (PR #4450), fixing client scroll bars (PR #4465), and correcting bunx/npx detection (PR #4463).

Other completed items include documentation updates to fix broken Eliza documentation links (PR #4460), a feature addition to include elizadb in .gitignore (PR #4464), and a maintenance task to remove plugin-redpill from the monorepo (PR #4428).

Over a two-day period (May 5-7, 2025), the elizaos/eliza repository showed consistent development activity. The repository had 15 new pull requests (9 merged), 2 new issues, and maintained an active contributor base that grew from 9 to 11 contributors.

Two issues have been reported in the elizaOS/eliza repository. Issue #4457 reports a 'pglite log entry unicode escape error' submitted by user plvo. Issue #4461 notes that 'settings.discord.autoPost is not implemented yet in eliza v2' and was submitted by user POKENA7.

The sources provide information about the top contributors for the GitHub repository elizaOS/eliza. However, both sources appear to be identical and contain only the heading 'Top contributors for elizaOS/eliza' without listing the actual contributors.
